title: Plumbing, Heating, and Air-Conditioning Contractors

alternatives.name: Scaled HVAC-led contractor with mixed project, retrofit, replacement, and service work
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the deterministic $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band; the dataset maps the band primarily to 100-to-499-employee firms, but verified normalized EBITDA controls when target financials are available.
Earns its largest activity share as a heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning contractor supplying specialty-trade labor and normally the installed equipment or parts.
Performs a mix that may include new installation, additions, renovation, replacement, maintenance, repair, and service for residential, commercial, institutional, or industrial customers.

May self-perform ductwork, sheet-metal installation, equipment setting, piping, testing, balancing, startup, project management, estimating, prefabrication, dispatch, or related mechanical scope when HVAC contracting remains primary.

Operates as an independent specialty contractor accountable for installed work or service delivery rather than solely as a labor broker, distributor, manufacturer, engineering consultancy, or captive facilities department.
exclusion_criteria: Plumbing-led, broad mechanical-led, process-piping or steam-fitting, building-sprinkler, refrigeration-specialist, or lawn-sprinkler businesses when those activities rather than HVAC contracting are primary.

Businesses focused primarily on HVAC controls wiring classified in 238210, duct cleaning classified in 561790, septic-system installation classified in 238910, or water-well work classified in 237110.
Manufacturers, wholesalers, equipment representatives, engineering-only firms, and facilities-management providers that do not primarily perform in-scope specialty construction.
Businesses whose verified normalized EBITDA is outside the $1 million to $10 million target band, regardless of employee count.
alternatives.rationale: HVAC is the largest published kind-of-business line at 40.9% of 238220 receipts. Applying that receipt share to the 1,152-firm deterministic band gives a transparent 471-firm base estimate. The range is widened because receipt share is not firm share and published data do not cross activity with enterprise size. NAPCS shows that new construction, maintenance and repair, and alterations are all material, so the selected archetype retains a mixed-work business model rather than asserting an unsupported pure-project or pure-service majority.

alternatives.name: Scaled plumbing-led contractor with mixed project and service work
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the deterministic target band and earns its largest activity share from plumbing installation, replacement, repair, or service.
May serve residential, commercial, institutional, or industrial customers and may include HVAC or mechanical work as a secondary line.
exclusion_criteria: HVAC-led, broad mechanical-led, sprinkler-specialist, refrigeration-specialist, or pipe-fitting-led firms.
Businesses outside the verified normalized EBITDA target band or primarily classified outside 238220.
alternatives.rationale: Plumbing is the second-largest official kind-of-business line at 25.2% of receipts; applying that marginal share to the deterministic band yields the 291-firm base, with a wide range for size and mixed-line uncertainty.

alternatives.name: Scaled broad mechanical contractor
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the deterministic target band and bids or delivers integrated mechanical packages as its primary activity.
Typically combines HVAC, plumbing, piping, sheet metal, equipment, coordination, fabrication, or related scopes under project accountability.
exclusion_criteria: Firms whose largest primary line is separately identifiable HVAC-only, plumbing-only, sprinkler, refrigeration, or pipe-fitting work.
General building contractors and engineering-only firms.
alternatives.rationale: Mechanical contracting represents 18.3% of official kind-of-business receipts, corresponding to a 210-firm receipt-share allocation of the deterministic band. Mixed scopes make overlap with HVAC and plumbing especially material.

alternatives.name: Scaled sprinkler, refrigeration, steam or pipe-fitting, or related specialty systems contractor
inclusion_criteria: Falls within the deterministic target band and has building sprinklers, refrigeration, steam or pipe fitting, lawn sprinklers, or another separately reported 238220 specialty as its primary activity.
Performs accountable installation or service rather than only selling equipment or labor.
exclusion_criteria: HVAC-led, plumbing-led, or broad mechanical-led firms already assigned to another enumerated archetype.
Waterless fire suppression repair without installation, commercial refrigeration repair without installation, and other work explicitly cross-referenced outside 238220.
alternatives.rationale: Building sprinkler, lawn sprinkler, refrigeration, and steam or pipe-fitting lines together represent about 8.6% of published kind-of-business receipts, corresponding to a 99-firm base allocation. The grouped record is heterogeneous and is retained only to enumerate the material tail.

residual.name: Other, mixed, or unclassified target-band 238220 contractors
residual.rationale: Residual equals the 1,152-firm deterministic band less the receipt-share allocations to the four enumerated archetypes. It covers firms without a stable dominant line, other reported construction or secondary activities, and error from using receipts as a firm-count proxy.

sources.title: 2022 Statistics of U.S. Businesses annual tables
EC2223KOB Construction: Value of Business Done for Kind-of-Business
EC2200NAPCSINDPRD Selected Sectors: Industry by Products

target_archetype.selection_basis: The deterministic dataset estimates 1,152 target-band firms, mapping the band primarily to the 100-to-499 employee class with average receipts of about $49.9 million and implied EBITDA of about $4.0 million. EC2223KOB reports $297.609 billion of total 238220 receipts, led by HVAC at $121.738 billion (40.9%), plumbing at $75.078 billion (25.2%), and mechanical contracting at $54.365 billion (18.3%). The selected HVAC-led archetype is therefore the largest transparent receipt-share allocation of the target band, not the most attractive archetype. EC2200NAPCSINDPRD separately reports about $125.296 billion of new-construction services (42.1%), $87.222 billion of maintenance and repair (29.3%), and $78.955 billion of additions, renovations, and alterations (26.5%). Because all three work types are material and are not crossed with kind of business or size, the selected archetype is explicitly mixed-work rather than forced into an unsupported pure service or pure project class.

value_basis.scope: Independent HVAC-led mixed-work contractors in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band, typically approximated by the 100-to-499 employee size class.

labor_contractor_cash_cost_share.rationale: Class-neutral feasibility assumption for total cash paid to employees and labor subcontractors by a scaled HVAC-led contractor with mixed installation, retrofit, replacement, maintenance, and service work. The broad range permits materially different self-performance, subcontracting, customer, union, service, and project mixes and is not calibrated to a downstream result.
caveats: This is an authored bounded assumption, not a source-backed estimate or a broad-NAICS proxy.
The share includes employee cash compensation and labor-contractor cash cost but excludes equipment, materials, rent, vehicles, software, insurance, financing, taxes, and other nonlabor cash costs.
Subcontract agreements may bundle labor, materials, equipment, overhead, and profit; target diligence must separate those elements before replacing this assumption.
Actual cash-cost share may vary by self-performance, union status, geography, prevailing-wage exposure, project or service mix, and accounting classification.
Use of an ASSUMPTION/NONE basis forces the resulting evidence state to UNPROVEN until target-specific source evidence replaces it.

roles.title: Field installation, replacement, maintenance, and service execution
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive delivery bucket for employee and subcontractor cash cost directly executing onsite installation, replacement, commissioning, preventive maintenance, diagnosis, and repair; excludes shop fabrication, logistics, supervision, design, selling, and administration assigned below.

roles.title: Shop fabrication, warehouse, fleet, and jobsite logistics
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive bucket for labor producing or staging ductwork and assemblies and moving tools, equipment, and materials; excludes onsite installers and technicians.

roles.title: Field supervision and project management
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive bucket for foremen, superintendents, construction or service managers, and project managers accountable for crews, schedules, budgets, quality, safety, coordination, and closeout; excludes hands-on delivery hours classified above.

roles.title: Estimating, engineering, detailing, and technical coordination
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive preconstruction and technical bucket for estimating, takeoff, bidding, engineering, BIM or CAD detailing, procurement coordination, and related technical planning.

roles.title: Sales, dispatch, scheduling, and customer operations
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive commercial and service-operations bucket for business development, account management, call handling, dispatch, scheduling, and customer coordination not performed as project management.

roles.title: Executive management, finance, human resources, compliance, and administration
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive corporate-support bucket for executive management and office functions including finance, accounting, payroll, human resources, compliance, legal coordination, information technology, and general administration.

value_basis.rationale: This bounded role structure exists only to make a class-neutral feasibility run operational when no target-specific employee-plus-contractor cash-cost source is available. Roles are fixed, mutually exclusive allocations of the assumed cash-cost pool and sum to one. No source, broad-industry occupation mix, target observation, or downstream outcome is claimed as support.
caveats: Role weights are assumptions, not measured employment, hours, payroll, contractor, or fully burdened cost shares.
The structure does not distinguish union from nonunion labor, geography, residential from nonresidential customers, or project from service operating units.
Owner compensation, temporary labor, bundled subcontractor invoices, and shared corporate services may be classified differently in target accounts.
Target-specific payroll, contractor ledgers, job-cost reports, and quality-of-earnings evidence must replace both the aggregate share and role weights when available.
This value basis must be treated as UNPROVEN and may not be upgraded merely because the assumed numbers are internally coherent.

source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau, 2022 NAICS definition for 238220: https://www.census.gov/naics/?details=238220&input=238220&year=2022
source_hints.why: Defines installation and servicing of plumbing, heating, and air-conditioning equipment; lists HVAC, mechanical, refrigeration, sprinkler, ductwork, piping, and related activities; and identifies important cross-industry exclusions.

source_hints.area: firm population and deterministic target-band scale
source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau, 2022 Statistics of U.S. Businesses annual tables: https://www.census.gov/data/tables/2022/econ/susb/2022-susb-annual.html
source_hints.why: Provides official firms, establishments, employment, payroll, and receipts by enterprise size and supports reconciliation of the 1,152-firm target-band estimate to the 100-to-499 employee class.

source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau, 2022 Economic Census EC2223KOB, Construction: Value of Business Done for Kind-of-Business: https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/economic-census/data/2022/sector23/EC2223KOB.zip
source_hints.why: Reports 238220 receipts separately for HVAC, plumbing, mechanical, building sprinkler, lawn sprinkler, refrigeration, steam and pipe fitting, and other contractor activities; HVAC is the largest reported line.

source_hints.source: U.S. Census Bureau, 2022 Economic Census EC2200NAPCSINDPRD, Selected Sectors: Industry by Products for the U.S. and States: https://data.census.gov/table/ECNNAPCSIND2022.EC2200NAPCSINDPRD
source_hints.why: Separates industry revenue across new construction, additions or renovations, and maintenance or repair, with residential and nonresidential building categories; new construction is the largest broad work type.

source_hints.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, May 2023 OEWS for NAICS 238220: https://www.bls.gov/oes/2023/may/naics5_238220.htm
source_hints.why: Provides the official industry-wide employment and wage mix needed to test any later target-specific role evidence, while remaining explicitly a broad-industry proxy rather than the accepted role basis.

source_hints.source: Target-firm quality-of-earnings reports, audited or reviewed financial statements, payroll extracts, union and nonunion labor reports, WIP schedules, job-cost ledgers, and subcontractor-cost detail for HVAC-led nonresidential project contractors in the target band
source_hints.why: Needed to establish fully burdened labor share and cost-weighted field, supervision, project-management, estimating, engineering, fabrication, service, sales, and administrative roles without substituting the heterogeneous NAICS average.

source_hints.source: Census microdata or a reproducible firm census jointly classifying 238220 target-band firms by primary kind of business, residential versus nonresidential work, new versus retrofit versus maintenance work, enterprise size, self-performance, and contract model
source_hints.why: Needed to quantify the selected archetype against residential HVAC service, plumbing-led, mechanical, retrofit, maintenance, sprinkler, refrigeration, piping, and mixed-service alternatives; published official tables provide only separate marginal distributions.

commercial_retention: What share of target-archetype revenue and gross profit comes from fixed-price bids, negotiated projects, time-and-material work, service agreements, replacements, change orders, and other arrangements?

How frequently are project bids and service prices reset, and how do customer contracts treat labor-hour reductions, unit pricing, allowances, shared savings, escalation, change orders, warranties, retainage, and liquidated damages?
How much work is self-performed versus subcontracted, and which party retains the economic effect of changes in field hours, rework, procurement, prefabrication, scheduling, or project duration?
How do realized price, gross margin, backlog conversion, labor productivity, change-order recovery, and customer retention vary across comparable jobs over an approximately 24-month period?
implementation: What proportion of matching firms has governed production use of integrated estimating, BIM or detailing, dispatch, field service, project management, job costing, procurement, document control, and customer-management systems?
Which workflows are actually used across a material share of jobs rather than merely licensed, piloted, or used by isolated employees?
What implementation time, data cleanup, system integration, training, review burden, rework, cybersecurity constraint, and recurring vendor cost are observed for representative target-band firms?
What evidence supports the timing estimate for majority material use among competing firms, segmented between project construction and service operations?
sale_availability: What share of the deterministic target band matches the selected HVAC-led nonresidential project archetype after separating residential service, plumbing-led, broad mechanical, sprinkler, refrigeration, piping, and mixed alternatives?

How many economically distinct U.S. firms in the target band are independent rather than subsidiaries, sponsor-backed add-ons, branches, captive construction divisions, or union signatory entities under common control?

How many strategic buyers and sponsor-backed platforms have completed acquisitions of matching firms within a stated recent period, without counting multiple brands under one buyer as distinct demand?
What seller-supply, succession, licensing, bonding, union, customer-concentration, key-estimator, key-project-manager, and owner-dependence conditions are documented directly for matching targets?
valuation_robustness: What disclosed entry multiples apply to independent HVAC-led nonresidential project contractors with $1 million to $10 million EBITDA, excluding owner-operator SDE transactions, residential service platforms, and substantially larger public contractors?

How do project versus service mix, backlog quality, percentage-of-completion accounting, WIP underbillings or overbillings, bonded capacity, customer concentration, union status, prefabrication, geography, and management depth affect observed terms?
What independently supportable exit multiple applies to the same size, accounting basis, and business model without importing residential recurring-service or public-company premiums?

Are enterprise value, assumed debt, working-capital requirements, equipment, real estate, earn-outs, rollover equity, seller financing, and acquired cash normalized consistently in comparable transactions?
operator_survival: At mature technology use, which customer needs still require a licensed and bonded operator accountable for site conditions, coordination, installation quality, safety, code compliance, commissioning, warranty, and project delivery risk?

Which revenue lines are most exposed to general-contractor self-performance, manufacturer service networks, facilities-team insourcing, lower-cost specialty contractors, prefab specialists, or integrated mechanical competitors?
Can the target remain an independent accountable contractor through construction cycles, labor shortages, project delays, equipment lead times, retainage, claims, and working-capital stress?
How do maintenance, repair, retrofit, replacement, and recurring service operations alter survival compared with a predominantly new-construction backlog?

special_notes: This class-neutral specification defines only the industry, target, evidence, and research boundaries; it contains no downstream outcome estimates or labels.

The selected archetype is the largest estimated target-band archetype, not the most attractive one. Selection follows HVAC as the largest official kind-of-business line, while the mixed-work definition reflects that new construction, maintenance and repair, and additions or renovations are all material official work types.

The Census kind-of-business, NAPCS product, and enterprise-size tables are separate marginal distributions. They do not prove that HVAC-led firms are 40.9% of target-band firm count or that any one HVAC work model is a majority of the 1,152 target-band estimate.

Material alternatives include plumbing-led contractors; broad mechanical contractors; residential HVAC repair, replacement, and membership-service operators; nonresidential retrofit and maintenance contractors; building-sprinkler contractors; refrigeration specialists; steam and process-piping contractors; and mixed HVAC-plumbing-mechanical firms.

Overlap is substantial because one establishment may report HVAC, plumbing, mechanical, installation, renovation, and maintenance receipts. Later coverage work must classify firms by their largest economic activity and retain mixed-firm uncertainty rather than adding marginal shares as though disjoint.
The deterministic n_band is an industry-wide estimate based on a common margin and size-class averages; it must not be relabeled as a count of firms matching the selected archetype.

The Economic Census reports $297.609 billion of 238220 receipts while the SUSB-derived dataset reports $306.277 billion; source-program and universe differences must remain visible rather than forcing exact reconciliation.
The industry occupational mix and dataset labor share remain labeled proxies only. No numeric target-specific role or labor basis is asserted in this specification.
